issues.comment: isConditionalPasskeysEnabled() is called with zero arguments, but the only definition of this method (in parent class UsernamePasswordForm) requires a UserModel parameter: `protected boolean isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user)`. No zero-arg overload exists, so this is a compilation error. The likely intention was `isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(context.getUser())`.

The `user != null` condition in isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(UserModel user) is inverted relative to the old behavior. The old code filled passkeys context specifically when user was null (initial login — the else branch of `if (context.getUser() != null)`). The new code gates it on isConditionalPasskeysEnabled(context.getUser()), which returns false when user is null. This means the initial login page (before any user is identified) will not have the passkeys conditional UI set up — the autocomplete="username webauthn" form won't get the WebAuthn challenge, rpId, etc. Similarly, the old challenge(context, error, field) method filled passkeys data when context.getUser() == null, but the new version fills only when user is not null. The fix should change the condition to something like `(currentUser == null || currentUser.credentialManager().isConfiguredFor(...))`.
